page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX instructions. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it offers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i-flex.8xlarge demonstrates strong performance in several areas. Its Geekbench single-core score of 2153.0 and multi-core score of 17709.0 are rated as strong, alongside a top-tier result in text processing at 2873.0. The server excels in data compression and decompression, achieving a strong 33.95 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression and 222.99 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 36151.65. For machine learning workloads, the instance shows strong prompt processing speeds of 2512.5 tokens/sec for a small 135M LLM and 207.68 tokens/sec for a medium 7B LLM, with top-tier text generation on the small model at 377.41 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i-flex family offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile optimized for raw processing power without the overhead of bundled local NVMe storage. This design reduces costs for workloads that rely on network-attached storage rather than local ephemeral disks. However, the lack of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a tradeoff, making it unsuitable for heavy local disk I/O or GPU-accelerated deep learning.

This instance is well-suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, image processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations. It is also highly capable for lightweight LLM inference tasks and cryptographic processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established at an 8 Gbps baseline. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-core and multi-core operations, particularly in text processing, data decompression, and cryptographic tasks. It also delivers strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM inference. This hardware profile is highly efficient for compute-bound workloads, including software compilation, static web serving, image processing, and lightweight machine learning inference, where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for text processing, cryptography, and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding processing workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor
64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Strong performance in text processing, cryptography, and data decompression
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference
